label: LSTMs & GRUs

node.label: LSTMs & GRUs
node.definition: LSTMs and GRUs use learned gates to regulate writing, forgetting, and exposing recurrent state, reducing the vanishing-gradient limitations of basic RNNs.
node.why_it_matters: The role of LSTMs & GRUs is to connect Recurrent neural networks, Gating & multiplicative control to downstream capabilities including xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids, Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection.
examples: substrate for Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Recurrent neural networks, Gating & multiplicative control.

dependencies.rationale: LSTMs & GRUs extends or specializes the architecture family Recurrent neural networks.
LSTMs & GRUs is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.
LSTMs & GRUs is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Differentiable memory read/write.
